etymology.description: Depicts a flag on a pole with a decoration on top -> stylized as 方(㫃)
etymology.interpretation: The character 㫃 originally depicted a flag on a pole, complete with a decorative element on top. Over time, it was stylized into its current form with the flag and pole disconnected and the image abstracted as 方(㫃). Although it shares visual similarities with characters like 朝(朝) (cháo, "morning"), it is unrelated to them in meaning.

examples.chinese: 他站在城楼上挥动㫃(他站在城樓上揮動㫃)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zhàn zài chénglóu shàng huīdòng bì.
examples.english: He waved the flag on the city gate tower.

examples.chinese: 大风把祭典上的㫃吹得猎猎作响(大風把祭典上的㫃吹得獵獵作響)。
examples.pinyin: Dàfēng bǎ jìdiǎn shàng de bì chuī dé liè liè zuò xiǎng.
examples.english: The strong wind made the flag at the ceremony flutter noisily.
